title: Goniopteris penangiana
intro: Goniopteris penangiana is a species of fern, specifically a type of polypod fern, native to Southeast Asia and characterized by its unique, lance-shaped leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Goniopteris penangiana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Goniopteris penangiana is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Goniopteris penangiana is classified within the family Gleicheniaceae. This family belongs to the order Gleicheniales, which is a part of the division Pteridophyta. The division Pteridophyta is also known as the ferns, and Goniopteris penangiana is a type of fern. Further classification places Goniopteris penangiana within the class Polypodiopsida. It is a member of the genus Goniopteris.
